enonce.texte: Transform the following direct speech sentences into reported speech. Pay attention to tense changes, pronoun changes, and time/place adverb changes.

questions.texte: Convert these statements from direct to indirect speech.

sousQuestions.texte: She said, 'I am going to the market.'
sousQuestions.reponse: She said (that) she was going to the market.

sousQuestions.texte: He said, 'I have finished my homework.'
sousQuestions.reponse: He said (that) he had finished his homework.

sousQuestions.texte: They said, 'We will come to the party.'
sousQuestions.reponse: They said (that) they would come to the party.

sousQuestions.texte: She said, 'I can speak French fluently.'
sousQuestions.reponse: She said (that) she could speak French fluently.

enonce.texte: Transform the following direct questions into reported speech. For wh-questions, keep the question word; for yes/no questions, use 'if' or 'whether'.

questions.texte: Convert these questions into reported speech.

sousQuestions.texte: She asked, 'Are you coming to the meeting?'
sousQuestions.reponse: She asked if/whether I was coming to the meeting.

sousQuestions.texte: The teacher asked, 'Why are you late?'
sousQuestions.reponse: The teacher asked why I was late.

sousQuestions.texte: He asked, 'Did you see the accident?'
sousQuestions.reponse: He asked if/whether I had seen the accident.

enonce.texte: Transform the following direct commands and requests into reported speech using 'tell' (for orders) and 'ask' (for requests) + to-infinitive.

questions.texte: Convert these commands and requests into reported speech.

sousQuestions.texte: She said, 'Please help me with this suitcase.' (request)
sousQuestions.reponse: She asked me to help her with that suitcase.

sousQuestions.texte: The teacher said, 'Close your books now.' (order)
sousQuestions.reponse: The teacher told us to close our books then.

sousQuestions.texte: He said, 'Don't touch the hot pan.' (prohibition)
sousQuestions.reponse: He told me not to touch the hot pan.

sousQuestions.texte: She said, 'Could you open the window, please?' (polite request)
sousQuestions.reponse: She asked me to open the window.

enonce.texte: Read the direct speech dialogue below and rewrite it as a reported speech paragraph. Use appropriate reporting verbs, backshift tenses, and adjust time/place expressions.

questions.texte: Rewrite this dialogue in reported speech.

sousQuestions.texte:
Dialogue:

John said, 'I am very tired today.'

Mary replied, 'You should go to bed early.'

John said, 'I have an important exam tomorrow.'

Mary said, 'I will help you study.'

John said, 'Thank you so much!'
sousQuestions.reponse: John said that he was very tired that day. Mary replied that he should go to bed early. John said that he had an important exam the next day. Mary said that she would help him study. John thanked her very much.

enonce.texte: You are a journalist covering a community event. A witness told you the following. Report what the witness said in a news report style.

questions.texte: Report the witness's statements in a coherent paragraph.

sousQuestions.texte:
Witness statement:

'I was walking home when I saw a car accident. The driver wasn't paying attention. I called the police immediately. The police arrived within five minutes. They helped the injured people. The accident was very serious.'
sousQuestions.reponse: The witness reported that he/she had been walking home when he/she had seen a car accident. He/she said that the driver hadn't been paying attention. He/she added that he/she had called the police immediately. He/she stated that the police had arrived within five minutes and that they had helped the injured people. He/she concluded that the accident had been very serious.

enonce.texte: This exercise contains a mix of reported statements, questions, and commands. Apply all the rules you have learned.

questions.texte: Transform the following mixed direct speech into reported speech.

sousQuestions.texte:
Mixed sentences:

1. He said, 'I don't know the answer.'

2. She asked, 'Where is the nearest hospital?'

3. The manager said, 'Finish this report today.'

4. She said, 'I can't come to the party because I'm busy.'

5. He asked, 'Did you enjoy the movie?'
sousQuestions.reponse:
1. He said that he didn't know the answer.

2. She asked where the nearest hospital was.

3. The manager told me to finish that report that day.

4. She said that she couldn't come to the party because she was busy.

5. He asked if I had enjoyed the movie.
